results.title: Analysis of Wealth Distribution Breakdown by Age Cohort
results.summary: The Ministry for Finance did not publish a decomposition of wealth inequality by asset class. Regarding the request for a breakdown by age cohort, the Minister stated this question was already addressed in the answers to Questions 1 and 2 during the Parliament Sitting on 25 February 2026. No new data or timeline for providing such a breakdown was announced in this session. The specific details of the previous answers are not included in this transcript.

results.title: Annual Report Publication of Non-Profit Healthcare Clusters for Public Access
results.summary: Public healthcare clusters operate on a not-for-profit basis, incurring deficits to subsidize services that are covered by Government grants. While annual revenue and expenditure details are not published, the public can verify financial positions via the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority's website. The Minister clarified that any post-grant surplus is reinvested into infrastructure and service improvements rather than distributed as profit. No timeline for resuming the publication of financial results in annual reports was provided.

results.title: Assessment of Progress in Placement of Fresh Graduates under Graduate Industry Traineeships Scheme
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed Members to refer to the reply provided to Ms He Ting Ru's written Parliamentary Question on 25 February 2026 for details regarding the Graduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) scheme. That prior response addressed the placement status of 350 fresh graduates as of February 2026, the timeline for filling all 800 available places, and the percentage of placements with small and medium enterprises. No new data or updates were presented in this session.

results.title: Adjusting Labour and Urban Planning Policies to Mitigate Heat-related Productivity Losses
results.summary: In September 2024, the Ministry of Manpower updated the heat stress management framework to include mandatory work-rest cycles for outdoor workers when the Wet Bulb Globe Temperature reaches 32°C or higher, requiring at least 10-minute hourly breaks for heavy physical tasks. Employers must also report work-related heat illnesses under the WSH Act, following a period from 2021 to 2025 that recorded 24 non-fatal cases, with 22 occurring outdoors. Beyond these operational mandates, the Government is implementing environmental modelling to identify wind corridors and planting trees for shade, alongside the Heat Stress Advisory to support community resilience. The Ministry commits to reviewing and enhancing these science-based measures to mitigate heat-related productivity losses while ensuring business continuity.

results.title: Ban on Flavoured Tobacco to Curb Appeal to Youths
results.summary: The Ministry of Health reviews tobacco control policies regularly and does not rule out future bans on flavoured tobacco products or further tax increases. While the Prime Minister recently raised cigarette tax, the Minister confirmed that menthol cigarettes currently account for nearly half of Singapore's market, a figure consistent with recent reports. International evidence indicates that flavours like menthol and fruit mask the harsh tobacco taste, increasing appeal to youths and potentially enhancing addiction. The Ministry is actively reviewing whether to introduce a ban on flavoured tobacco products to curb youth initiation, though no specific timeline or decision has been announced.

results.title: Breakdown of Direct School Admission Intake by Household Income
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry does not track the household income distribution of students admitted through the Direct School Admission (DSA) scheme. Consequently, specific percentages for students from bottom 20%, bottom 40%, or bottom 60% income families cannot be provided. This lack of data collection means the government has not published statistics on the socioeconomic composition of DSA intake.

results.title: Children of Unwed Mothers Excluded from Baby Bonus Cash Gift and Working Mother's Child Relief
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, an average of 745 non-marital live births occurred annually to Singapore Citizen mothers. The Baby Bonus Cash Gift and Working Mother's Child Relief were explicitly designed to encourage parenthood within marriage and were not extended to single unwed parents. The Ministry stated this exclusion is not driven by cost, noting that it does not track the hypothetical expense of extending these benefits nor compare it against downstream expenditures on alternative support pathways like ComCare, ComLink+, KidSTART, or public housing. Single unwed parents remain eligible for other Government benefits including leave provisions, infant care subsidies, education and healthcare subsidies, the Migrant Domestic Worker Levy Concession, and Child Development Account benefits. Additional financial assistance is available via the SupportGoWhere portal and family service centres.

results.title: Clarification by Minister of State for Health
results.summary: Subsidies and Pioneer Generation benefits are extended to patients at National Dental Centre Singapore (NDCS) for complex cases requiring specialist management, while polyclinics cover denture fabrication, repair, and fitting. A specific patient's mother received no subsidy because she was classified as a private patient; the Ministry of Health will contact the public to explain this situation. The Ministry recently announced higher subsidies for root canal treatments and permanent crowns and is continuously reviewing measures to support patients. Regarding MediSave coverage for dental subsidies, the Ministry is reviewing expansions but has not yet specified the extent of current coverage or confirmed future expansions.

results.title: Clarification by Prime Minister and Minister for Finance
results.summary: Mr Lawrence Wong issued a factual correction to his previous reply regarding the Domestic Recruitment Cap (DRC) for the Food and Beverage (F&B) sector. He clarified that lowering the DRC for F&B will inevitably lead to many F&B foreign workers in Singapore undertaking non-F&B jobs. This statement addresses concerns raised during the Debate on the Annual Budget Statement at the Sitting of 26 February 2026. The correction emphasizes the direct operational consequence of adjusting foreign worker quotas without specifying a new timeline or alternative measures.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for Finance
results.summary: Senior Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister for Finance, Mr Shawn Huang Wei Zhong, clarified that the Ministry of Finance is collaborating with GovTech to evaluate AI tools for use within Statutory Boards, agencies, and Ministries. Any tools deemed useful will be opened for internal use and subsequently made available to other businesses. This statement corrects a previous reply given during the Committee of Supply for Head M on 26 February 2026. The specific AI tools under study and the timeline for their release remain to be determined.

results.title: Committee of Supply Reporting Progress
results.summary: The Committee of Supply reported progress on the Estimates of Expenditure for FY2026/2027 and requested leave to resume proceedings the following day. No specific expenditure figures, programme details, or policy measures were disclosed during this brief update. The committee will continue its work on the budget estimates in the next session.

results.title: Cost-benefit Analysis for Transitioning from Incinerating Recyclables to Smart-bin System
results.summary: The Minister stated the National Environment Agency deploys smart bins within Public Waste Collection contracts and extended producer responsibility schemes for e-waste and beverage containers to aggregate clean-stream recyclables. These measures complement the existing blue bin system and public education initiatives aimed at reducing contamination. The Ministry will continue exploring ways to enhance the recycling ecosystem through community partnerships to collect more clean-stream recyclables over time. No specific cost-benefit analysis results or logistical benchmarks for a full transition to a segregated smart-bin system were provided in the response.

results.title: Data Centres' Use of Fresh Water Supply
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that most water supplied to data centres is NEWater rather than freshwater. Consequently, specific figures for freshwater usage by data centres in the past year are unavailable. The Ministry has not provided latest estimates for absolute volume or proportion to the national freshwater supply. The response clarifies the primary water source but does not quantify the remaining freshwater consumption or offer alternative data.

results.title: Data Integrity of Bus Waiting Time and Occupancy in ETA System Following January 2026 Glitch
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that data used for bus planning, including travel times and occupancy rates, is generated by a separate fleet management system. Consequently, the recent January 2026 glitch affecting the Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) system did not impact these specific datasets. No rectification of retrospective data or re-assessment of strategic planning is required as the affected ETA system is distinct from the operational data sources used for service planning.

results.title: Data on Premature Work Permit Cancellations in Last Five Years
results.summary: Work Permits in Singapore are valid for up to 24 months, with employers cancelling them early for reasons such as worker unsuitability or personal needs. Data from 2021 to 2025 indicates that approximately one-third of all issued or renewed Work Permits are cancelled before their expiry date. The Minister provided a breakdown of these premature cancellations across six-month bands prior to renewal, though specific percentage figures for each band were not detailed in the provided text.

results.title: Difference in Requirements for European Third Language Versus Asian Third Language Options Offered to Secondary 1 Students
results.summary: The Foreign Language programme offers French, German, Japanese, and Spanish as third languages to Secondary 1 students who demonstrate strong core subject abilities, reflecting an intensive curriculum designed to prepare them for undergraduate courses in these languages. Conversely, Malay, Chinese, Arabic, and Bahasa Indonesia are offered with lower eligibility thresholds to foster understanding of Singapore's multicultural heritage and facilitate daily interactions. The Ministry attributes this differential in requirements to the distinct objectives and intensity levels of each programme rather than resource constraints.

results.title: Discriminatory Practices in Private Residential Rental Market and Measures to Address Discrimination
results.summary: The Council for Estate Agencies (CEA) mandates that property agents refrain from discriminatory advertisements based on nationality, race, religion, or group within the private residential rental market. Agents found breaching these guidelines face enforcement action, and the public may lodge complaints directly with the CEA. The Ministry has not yet studied the extent of such practices or proposed stronger legislative measures beyond existing regulatory guidelines.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head M (Ministry of Finance)
results.summary: The Ministry of Finance (MOF) maintains that clear, consistent rules provide a competitive edge while actively streamlining regulatory requirements to support business productivity. To enhance accessibility and cashflow for SMEs, MOF is refreshing the GeBIZ e-procurement portal, expanding Tender Lite to ICT procurement from April for contracts up to $1 million, and introducing an Innovative Procurement Partnership. The government spends approximately $30 billion annually on procurement with significant grants directed toward SMEs, though current grant application processing remains at 4–10 weeks. MOF conducts holistic assessments of benefits and costs, including economic, social, and environmental factors, to ensure value for money and prevent excessive reserve build-up in Statutory Boards. While reviewing the $20,000 personal income tax exemption threshold against updated median incomes, the ministry also considers technological improvements and consumer preferences when evaluating GST price display concessions for the F&B and hotel sectors.

results.title: Electric Vehicle Training for Bus Captains
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority will mandate a new compulsory course for all bus captains on safely operating electric buses, launching later this year. Developed by the Singapore Bus Academy and NTUC LearningHub with input from the National Transport Workers' Union and bus operators, this initiative complements existing training provided by operators. The measure supports LTA's target to replace 50% of the public bus fleet with electric buses by 2030. Specific details regarding the curriculum content and minimum training hours were not stated in the transcript.

results.title: Enhanced Funding or Subsidies for Elective Egg Freezing, IVF and Other Fertility Treatments for Older Couples
results.summary: The Prime Minister's Office is collaborating with the Ministry of Health to review fertility health policies, specifically examining financing support for Assisted Conception Procedures. This review encompasses potential subsidies for elective egg freezing and Preimplantation Genetic Testing for Aneuploidy (PGT-A) for older couples, as well as enhanced co-funding for In-Vitro Fertilisation (IVF) and Intrauterine Insemination (IUI). Further details regarding the scope and timeline of these planned policies will be shared by the Ministry when ready.

results.title: Ensuring Increase in Goods Vehicle Permit Fees for Foreign-registered Goods Vehicles is Not Passed to Consumers and Producers
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that the Goods Vehicle Permit (GVP) fee increase for foreign-registered vehicles effective 1 January 2027 aims to align costs with Singapore-registered vehicles. The Government stated that whether companies absorb these costs or pass them to consumers is a commercial decision, with no specific intervention planned to prevent price pass-through. The Minister noted that fee reviews occur periodically to reflect changes in ownership and usage costs.

results.title: Extension of TR76 E-commerce Guidelines for Social Commerce Transactions
results.summary: The Technical Reference 76 (TR76) on e-commerce transactions was updated in September 2025 to strengthen consumer trust, fair competition, and anti-scam measures, but it does not currently specifically cover social commerce transactions. While many TR76 practices regarding transparency and accurate listings apply to social commerce, the Singapore Code of Advertising Practice administered by the Advertising Standards Authority of Singapore also governs ethical advertising for social commerce content creators. The Government will continue to monitor social commerce developments and work with stakeholders to assess whether further specific guidance is necessary.

results.title: Extent of GenAI Use among Muslims to Obtain Religious Information and Instruction
results.summary: MUIS assesses that while Generative AI offers convenience, it carries risks when used as a primary source for religious guidance due to the need for scholarly interpretation. Consequently, MUIS adopts GenAI judiciously with safeguards, exemplified by IftaSG, an initiative under the "Fatwa Lab" that uses prompt engineering and curated datasets to enhance Fatwa research accuracy and speed while maintaining oversight by qualified asatizah. The agency prioritizes equipping the Muslim community to use AI tools wisely through ongoing capability building in AI and digital literacy across mosques and madrasahs. Additionally, madrasahs are exploring AI to supplement Arabic teaching, aid student assessment, and streamline lesson planning, ensuring the community remains discerning in its use of these tools.

results.title: Facilitating Islamic Legacy Planning Workshops Tailored to Indian Muslim Community
results.summary: MUIS collaborates with Indian Muslim Organisations via the Indian Muslim Community Steering Committee to facilitate Islamic Legacy Planning workshops and train community ambassadors. The agency currently works with relevant agencies to raise awareness through campaigns like the Islamic Legacy Planning Village. Later this year, MUIS will introduce the Tamil version of the ILP guidebook, "The Final Amanah," to improve resource accessibility for the Indian Muslim community. No specific plans for community-specific editions of the Islamic Legacy Planning Village were confirmed in the response.

results.title: Factors Behind 36.2% Increase in Young Inmates in Singapore Prisons in 2025
results.summary: The 36.2% rise in young inmates in 2025 was primarily driven by increased incarcerations for commercial crimes, specifically unlicensed moneylending and money mule offences. While assault rates remain low relative to other jurisdictions, the Singapore Prison Service (SPS) employs a multi-pronged strategy involving Restorative Practice activities and technology to manage incidents. Young inmates face tailored psychology-based correctional programmes that equip them with life skills such as emotional regulation and resistance to anti-social peer influence, with effectiveness measured through inmate motivation and attitude changes. Upstream prevention is coordinated by the National Committee on Preventing Offending and Re-offending, utilizing the MSF's YouthGO! Programme to engage at-risk youths and their parents. Additionally, youths arrested for minor offences access diversionary programmes in the community to understand consequences, identify triggers, and develop self-management plans to control impulses.

results.title: Financial Assistance for Parents Whose Infants are Enrolled in ECDA's Childminding Pilot Programme
results.summary: The ECDA's Childminding Pilot for infants offers subsidies reducing out-of-pocket costs to approximately $700 monthly for full-time usage, significantly below market rates of $1,200 to $2,800. Currently, over 80% of enrolled families utilize Child Development Account funds to cover remaining expenses, while the pilot operates at two centres serving up to 100 infants with supply-side subsidies only. The Ministry notes that lower-income households (per capita income ≤$1,500) are directed toward Government-supported preschools where means-tested subsidies and fee caps allow full-day care costs as low as $40 monthly. As this remains a pilot to explore scalability and inclusivity, no additional financial assistance schemes beyond CDA funds and operator subsidies are currently available for this specific programme.

results.title: Formal Training in Restorative Practices to Address Bullying in Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that all teachers receive training in restorative approaches to foster positive classroom cultures and address bullying. This curriculum covers building peer relationships, managing challenging behaviours, and facilitating conflict resolution. Key personnel are also trained to implement prevention measures and engage parents. Additional professional development may be sourced from external providers through pre-service and in-service courses. The ongoing review aims to better address bullying, with current training already incorporating these restorative strategies.

results.title: Funding Support and Capability Building Initiatives for Muslim Student Societies in IHLs
results.summary: MUIS provides grants to Muslim student societies in Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) for youth development programmes, including Islamic learning and community religious activities. Beyond direct funding, MUIS collaborates with agencies like MENDAKI and the People's Association to support the Malay/Muslim Youth Taskforce, which focuses on career development, essential skills, cultural identity, and national participation. Specific initiatives include an annual dialogue with the Mufti for IHL students, mosque talks, and leadership development programmes for Muslim youths. These efforts aim to address student needs while fostering welfare and engagement across various settings.

results.title: Government Funding in Lab-grown Seafood and Cultured Meat Sector
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) supports the lab-grown seafood and cultured meat sector through research and development (R&D) investments in higher learning institutions to build capabilities in process development and nutrition. Since 2024, SFA has awarded a total of $64 million to 21 multi-year projects under RIE2025. The Minister noted that while the global alternative protein industry faces hurdles like higher production costs and weaker consumer acceptance, agencies remain committed to supporting the sector's potential.

results.title: Government Support Provided to Sino-Singapore Jilin Food Zone under Bilateral Cooperation Framework
results.summary: The Sino-Singapore Jilin Food Zone (JFZ), established in 2008 as a private sector-led project, received technical assistance and training from the former Agri-Food and Veterinary Authority (AVA) focused on food safety and animal health. This support specifically facilitated the initiative to establish and maintain a Foot-and-Mouth Disease-Free Zone for meat production. The Ministry provided no further updates on the project's current operational status or specific food security outcomes, noting that JFZ remains a private sector-led initiative without additional government support.

results.title: Government's Assessment of Social Media's Efforts to Detect Child Sexual Exploitation and Abuse in Livestreams
results.summary: Under the Broadcasting Act and Online Criminal Harms Act, the Government can direct social media services and internet service providers to disable access to child sexual exploitation and abuse material. Designated Social Media Services must implement technologies and processes to proactively detect and swiftly remove such content, including in livestreams, while protecting users from online grooming. These services are required to submit annual online safety reports to the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA), detailing their measures to combat harmful content. IMDA publishes these reports on its website alongside its assessment of each designated service's performance in improving user safety.

results.title: Homeowners who Have Taken Three or More HDB Loans
results.summary: As of end December 2025, approximately 11,000 households are servicing their third or subsequent HDB housing loans. Between 2023 and 2025, about 1,000 households per year approached HDB for a third loan on a case-by-case basis for urgent housing needs. HDB does not consider previous loan tenures in its assessment, as these do not affect current credit standing or affordability. The Board provides up to two standard loans, with flexibility granted only when financing options are exhausted.

results.title: Implementing Additional Measures to Enhance Road Safety in Areas with High Pedestrian Footfall
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority will review the need for additional road safety measures in high pedestrian footfall areas following the recent Chinatown tragedy. This assessment is contingent upon the outcome of the Traffic Police's ongoing investigations. No specific measures, timelines, or eligibility criteria have been announced at this stage. The Minister confirmed that further action depends on the investigation results.

results.title: Introducing Locally Offered Degree in Veterinary Science
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that new undergraduate programmes at Autonomous Universities are introduced following consultation with relevant ministries and agencies to assess industry demand and national manpower needs. While no specific plan to introduce a locally offered veterinary science degree was confirmed, the Ministry will continue collaborating with sector agencies like the Ministry of National Development and the National Parks Board to monitor and assess strategies for meeting Singapore's long-term veterinary manpower requirements.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head U (Prime Minister's Office)
results.summary: The Government will establish a new Workgroup chaired by the Prime Minister to holistically address the declining Total Fertility Rate (TFR) through a four-pronged approach: enhancing support, cultivating positive mindsets, fostering family-friendly workplaces, and engaging society. This initiative aims to create an optimistic environment for parenthood rather than prescribing a single path, acknowledging that decisions on marriage and family are personal. The Prime Minister expressed confidence in the PMO team to drive systemic workplace reforms, including expanding Flexible Work Arrangements for SMEs and strengthening the pool of certified job redesign specialists. While the Government will continue to explore ways to ensure immigrant commitment and integration, it maintains that a stable citizen core is critical, noting that citizen population growth may slow to approximately 0.5% going forward.

results.title: LPA Form 1 Applications Aborted Due To Office of the Public Guardian's Online Portal Issues
results.summary: The Office of the Public Guardian (OPG) cannot track the specific number of aborted Lasting Power of Attorney Form 1 applications caused by online portal or SingPass issues, as the public does not report such incidents. To maintain application continuity during prolonged system unavailability, donors may submit hardcopy forms or seek assistance at ServiceSG centres. OPG has enhanced system resilience by migrating the OPG Online system to the Government Commercial Cloud and increasing database capacity ahead of the fee waiver expiry on 31 March 2026. The agency will continue exploring further measures to improve system stability as required.

results.title: Lessons Learnt from ETA System Disruption to Prevent System Failures to Other Critical Systems in Public Transport Infrastructure
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that lessons from the recent ETA system disruption will strengthen availability and accuracy standards. The defective software causing the issue has been fixed, and the system has stabilised. Critical systems affecting train and bus operations must now meet higher reliability standards and undergo more rigorous testing before deployment. These measures ensure that similar failures do not occur in other critical public transport infrastructure systems.

results.title: Linking Carbon Tax Increases to Technology Readiness or Regional Carbon Price Movements
results.summary: The Minister confirmed Singapore will maintain the planned carbon tax trajectory of $50 to $80 per tonne by 2030, explicitly rejecting a direct link to technology readiness or regional price alignment. This approach reflects Singapore's status as an alternative energy-disadvantaged nation where decarbonisation solutions incur higher costs and longer implementation lead times. The Government provides advance notice of the trajectory to enable business adaptation and will continually assess the post-2027 path to balance long-term resilience with nearer-term cost impacts on businesses and households. While alert to international developments, the current stance prioritises an economy-wide price signal to drive energy efficiency and investment in low-carbon solutions across all sectors.

results.title: Maintaining High Measles Vaccination Coverage Through Enhanced Vaccination Programmes
results.summary: Under the Infectious Diseases Act, measles vaccination is compulsory for all children aged 12 and below residing in Singapore, as well as foreign-born children under 12 applying for long-term passes. The Government maintains coverage through HealthHub notifications, reminder letters, preschool enrolment checks for immunity proof, and catch-up vaccination in primary and secondary schools. Officials attribute global infection spikes to waning vaccine trust and loss of herd immunity, urging the population to rely on scientific evidence. No specific timeline or new programme names were stated beyond existing compulsory measures.

results.title: NIE's Bachelor Programmes Acceptance Rate and Safeguarding Subject-specific Teacher Supply Shortages
results.summary: The National Institute of Education's Bachelor of Arts/Science admission rate rose from 12% to 18% over the past five years, with selection based on academic merit and teaching aptitude to address subject-specific manpower needs. Beyond these programmes, the Ministry recruits fresh graduates and mid-careerists through professional training diplomas and postgraduate degrees. To ensure teacher supply, the Ministry employs strategies including early talent development, the Teaching Internship Programme, Teacher Training Sponsorship, and Teaching Scholarships, while establishing new recruitment channels and enhancing outreach for subjects with greater needs.

results.title: Number of Overweight Students in Schools and Weight Management Programmes to Help Them
results.summary: Overweight and severely overweight student proportions in Singapore schools have declined from 16% in 2021 to a stable 13% from 2023, remaining lower than many international peers. Schools employ a holistic approach integrating Physical Education, inter-class games, and Co-curricular Activities to cultivate active lifestyles and healthy habits like nutritious eating. Complementing school efforts, the multi-ministry Grow Well SG strategy launched in January 2025 promotes healthy nutrition, sleep, screen use, and physical activity. Under this initiative, every child receives a personalised Health Plan with lifestyle prescriptions, while the Health Promotion Board guides students through annual School Health Screenings and supports parents with practical resources to foster consistent behaviour change for long-term weight management.

results.title: Number of Preschools that Offer Care Services Beyond 7.00 pm
results.summary: Currently, around 20 preschools offer extended care beyond 7.00 pm, a decrease from 40 in 2021, as operators treat extended hours as a business decision subject to staffing and resource constraints. The Minister emphasized that while preschools can charge for extra hours, demand has likely declined due to increased flexible work arrangements and parental preference for quality family time over extended preschool stays. Alternative support includes paid or community-based babysitting services, alongside the 2024 Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangements to encourage employers to support caregiving needs. The Ministry does not mandate a specific number of extended-hour preschools per estate, noting that rigid cut-off times may not be the primary driver of inequality compared to workforce flexibility.

results.title: Operational Protocols to Safeguard Local Healthcare Workers in Light of Overseas Nipah Virus Infections
results.summary: Public and private hospitals in Singapore have established infection prevention and control protocols applicable to the Nipah virus, developed from experience with SARS, H1N1, and COVID-19. These measures include isolation facilities and adequate supplies of personal protective equipment. Under the Infectious Diseases Act, further contingency measures can be quickly activated to protect healthcare staff. The Ministry of Health will continue to review outbreak response plans and conduct regular exercises with healthcare institutions to ensure service continuity and staff safety during a potential outbreak.

results.title: Otter Population Control and Management Strategies in Residential Estates
results.summary: NParks manages otter populations through a science- and community-based approach involving the Otter Working Group. Current measures include trapping and relocating otters from high-conflict zones and using olfactory deterrents to guide them away from residential areas. The Ministry is also studying sterilisation as a potential long-term population control option. Residents are not currently relieved of implementing their own defensive measures, as the strategy focuses on joint management rather than removing all financial or logistical burdens from the community.

results.title: Debate on Annual Budget Statement
results.summary: Budget 2026 prioritizes workforce adaptability and societal unity, acknowledging that while growth is essential, it must be inclusive to address structural headwinds like AI displacement and global volatility. The Government clarifies that heavy fiscal levers currently focus on AI adoption and deployment, noting Singapore cannot compete in compute wars but should instead identify niche advantages through Edge AI and responsible implementation rules. Regarding healthcare, rising waiting times are framed as symptoms of increased demand in a more capable system rather than capacity failures, with sustainability dependent on managing demand and strengthening primary care. On seniors, the Government supplements cash support via CPF/MediSave top-ups and Silver Support, while addressing digitalisation barriers and reviewing means-testing criteria for housing costs. The R&D ecosystem requires institutional reform to consolidate funding and create a market for outcomes, moving beyond grant-giving structures that discourage risk-taking.

results.title: Oversight of Wakaf Management
results.summary: Under the Administration of Muslim Law Act, all wakaf assets are owned by MUIS and managed by Warees Investments and appointed mutawallis to ensure financial sustainability and adherence to donor intentions. Governance includes MUIS Council-approved mutawallis, periodic audits, and oversight by the MUIS Wakaf Disbursement Committee. In 2024, $11.3 million in rental returns were disbursed to beneficiaries such as mosques, madrasahs, and charities, with funds used for maintenance, welfare, and community development. MUIS and Warees conduct asset enhancement and redevelopment, exemplified by projects at The Red House and Jamae Chulia, while engaging community leaders to balance heritage value with planning constraints.

results.title: Population Threshold Parameters for Placement of HDB Estate Dialysis Centres Across Singapore
results.summary: As of February 2026, 56 subsidised community dialysis centres operate across Singapore, with distribution determined by projected patient demand rather than fixed population thresholds. The Ministry of Health also factors in site availability, public transport accessibility, and co-location synergy with other community facilities when siting new centres. The Minister confirmed that specific planning parameters or population thresholds for placement do not currently exist, and no timeline for developing them was stated.

results.title: Positive Outcomes from Restriction Orders Issued since July 2025 and Expanding Scope to Other Financial Service Providers
results.summary: As of 15 February 2026, 12 Restriction Orders (ROs) have been issued under the Protection from Scams Act, with nine currently in effect. These orders apply exclusively to bank accounts and credit facilities, as these channels are deemed sufficient to significantly reduce the risk of victims transferring funds to scammers. The Ministry for Home Affairs has determined there is no immediate need to expand the scope of ROs to cryptocurrency exchanges, remittance companies, or e-wallet providers. While the review of expanding the scope to other financial service providers is ongoing, the Monetary Authority of Singapore will continue monitoring the situation to consider future adjustments if necessary.

results.title: Proportion and Career Progression of Female Foreign Service Officers, and Request for Publication of Gender-disaggregated Appointment Data
results.summary: Since 2022, approximately half of Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) employees are women, while the proportion of women in senior appointments has remained stable at about a quarter. The Minister acknowledged that overseas postings are disruptive for officers with families, particularly female officers with greater caregiving responsibilities. Consequently, the Ministry regularly reviews human resources policies to support career aspirations and family obligations, including considering personal circumstances for postings and providing support for accompanying spouses and children. All new hires are informed that MFA careers involve periodic relocations, and selections are based on merit. The Minister did not specify exact timelines for policy changes or confirm the publication of annual gender-disaggregated appointment data.

results.title: Proportion of Advanced Practice Nurses in Workforce and Plans for Their Development
results.summary: As of 31 December 2025, there were 419 Advanced Practice Nurses (APNs) in active practice, representing 1% of Singapore's nursing workforce. The Ministry of Health targets training 700 APNs by 2030 to support care delivery across acute hospitals, community settings, mental health services, primary care, home care, and nursing homes. APNs, who hold post-graduate training, are empowered to make specific clinical decisions and collaborate with doctors to provide complex nursing care. The Minister did not specify new clinical specialties or roles currently under consideration for certification.

results.title: Proportion of Muslim Couples Attending Marriage Preparation Programme
results.summary: On average, one in seven first-time Muslim couples attended the Cinta Abadi Marriage Preparation Programme since 2021. The Ministry of Social and Family Development offers a $170 rebate to eligible Singaporean couples attending their first approved programme. Full fee subsidies are available for eligible low-income households regardless of education status. No additional fee waivers or rebates are provided specifically for couples in full-time education beyond these existing measures.

results.title: Protection Measures for Vaccination-ineligible Infants Against Measles Outbreak in Singapore
results.summary: Measles cases in Singapore are driven by a global resurgence and imported risks rather than sustained local transmission. Infants under 12 months remain vulnerable as they are not yet eligible for vaccination. Protection relies on maintaining high population immunity through robust surveillance and monitoring vaccination coverage. The Ministry continues to respond to cases while addressing pockets of unvaccinated individuals and high travel volumes from outbreak areas. No additional specific measures beyond these existing strategies were announced for this vulnerable group.

results.title: Public Complaints Towards Festive Surcharge Pricings by F&B Establishments over Past Five Years
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, the Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI) and the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) received a total of four complaints regarding the quantum and/or transparency of festive surcharges by retail and food establishments. Two complaints were lodged in 2021 and two in 2022, with no complaints recorded for subsequent years. In relevant cases, consumers were advised to seek assistance from the Consumers Association of Singapore, and no further follow-ups were required from MTI or CCS.

results.title: Reason for Change in Government's Definition of Household Income in Key Household Income Trends Report
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry explained that the redefinition of household income in the Key Household Income Trends (KHIT) report addresses Singapore's ageing demographics by including non-employment income sources like CPF annuities, rentals, and investments for retiree households. This revised methodology aligns with international practices, specifically the Canberra Group Handbook, to ensure comprehensive representation of resident households. To maintain time-series comparability, the new definition and expanded coverage were applied retrospectively to annual household income statistics from 2015 onwards, ensuring consistency in future KHIT reports.

results.title: Registering Employees Affected by Sudden Business Closures as Creditors to Recover Owed Salaries
results.summary: When a company enters bankruptcy, judicial management, or liquidation, employees with salary arrears must file a Proof of Debt with the liquidator under the Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act. The Tripartite Alliance for Dispute Management (TADM) supports this process via a Judicial Management Guide, a Chatbot, and advisory officers who assist claimants in completing documentation and contacting liquidators. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) investigates errant employers who willfully breach employment legislation during winding up to determine liability for unpaid salaries. MOM identifies the liable party as the entity employing the worker under a contract of service and managing their work, though a parent company may also be held liable if evidence shows it instigated, conspired, or intentionally aided the offence.

results.title: Retention of International Carbon Credits Limit and Risks of Industrial Relocation
results.summary: The Ministry retains the 5% cap on international carbon credits to ensure companies prioritize domestic abatement, aligning with jurisdictions like South Korea while considering the maturation of low-carbon solutions. Eligible Emissions-Intensive Trade-Exposed (EITE) sector companies receive transitory carbon tax allowances conditional on meeting efficiency targets and submitting decarbonisation plans to address near-term competitiveness. Additionally, firms may utilise existing grants, including the Resource Efficiency Grant for Emissions and the Energy Efficiency Grant, to fund energy efficiency investments. The Government will monitor international developments to balance climate ambitions with economic competitiveness during the transition.

results.title: Review of Alternative Pathways and Bridging Nursing Programmes for Qualified Singaporean Applicants
results.summary: The Minister clarified that passion for healthcare service is the primary criterion for nursing education, with current O-level cut-offs set at an ELR2B2 aggregate of 28 points to balance rigour and accessibility. While the Early Admissions Exercise prioritizes aptitude and motivation, the Ministry confirmed existing alternative pathways, including ITE training for Enrolled Nurses and mid-career skills re-development programmes, to address workforce needs. No immediate review of entry criteria was announced, but these flexible routes remain available for qualified Singaporeans seeking to enter the nursing profession.

results.title: Reviewing Structural Load Capacity of Multi-storey Car Parks Given Greater Adoption of EVs which are Generally Heavier
results.summary: All Housing and Development Board car parks are designed to support passenger vehicles with an unladen weight not exceeding 2,000 kilogrammes, and current structures remain within safety margins despite heavier electric vehicles. The Minister confirmed that existing standards provide sufficient safety for current and projected weight trends. Agencies are reviewing structural enhancements to accommodate potentially heavier vehicles in the future, with further details to be announced when ready. Older car parks constructed under earlier design codes were not explicitly distinguished regarding load capacity differences in the response.

results.title: Rising AI Automation Concerns Over Job Losses and Long-Term Livelihood and Job Preservation Measures for Singaporeans
results.summary: The Minister reported that Singapore's labour market remains resilient in 2025, with resident employment growth outpacing 2024 levels and unemployment and retrenchment rates holding steady at 2.8% and 1.5 per 1,000 employees respectively. To address AI-driven transformation, the Government has allocated over $400 million via the Enterprise Workforce Transformation Package (EWTP) to support businesses in redesigning roles, reskilling workers, and adopting AI technologies. Concurrently, the Ministry of Education is refreshing higher learning curricula, while SkillsFuture Singapore will streamline AI learning pathways and provide six months of free access to premium AI tools for selected training participants. Furthermore, SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore will merge into a new statutory board under Budget 2026 to deliver seamless career guidance, skills training, and job matching, complemented by expanded Career Conversion Programmes to facilitate transitions into growth sectors.

results.title: Role for Singapore in Global Space Economy and Developing Local Talent Pipeline for Space Systems and Satellite Operations
results.summary: Singapore aims to leverage its strengths in aerospace, microelectronics, and AI to participate in the global space economy, projected to reach US$1.8 trillion by 2035. The government will invest in niche capabilities like small synthetic aperture radar satellites, satellite quantum secure communications, and downstream applications for maritime and sustainability sectors. These high-growth areas are expected to generate economic value and skilled jobs in engineering, data science, and geospatial analytics. For talent development, existing pathways include university programmes, mid-career transitions from adjacent industries, and government schemes like the Industrial Postgraduate Programme and Space Technology Development Programme. The latter will transition to the National Space Agency of Singapore upon its establishment on 1 April 2026. Singapore also seeks to contribute to international and ASEAN cooperation, having hosted the Singapore Space Summit 2026 to strengthen partnerships.

results.title: SG Enable's Support for ITE Graduates with Disabilities to Access Host Companies under Work-Study Diploma Programme
results.summary: SG Enable supports ITE Work-Study Diploma graduates with disabilities by deploying trained job coaches to foster workplace integration and advising employers on job redesign. The agency also connects inclusive employers, such as those holding the SG Enable Enabling Mark, with ITE schools and provides disability awareness training. While a centralised repository of inclusive employers was suggested to aid schools in finding host organisations for diplomas and attachments, the Ministry noted that matching remains a bespoke process tailored to individual student strengths and interests. Regarding programme deadline extensions for graduates unable to secure hosts, the Ministry indicated it would explore the suggestion but emphasized that each case requires a customised assessment of possibilities rather than a blanket policy change.

results.title: Securing Singapore’s Critical Information Infrastructure Dependent on Satellite and Space Systems
results.summary: Over 99% of Singapore's internet traffic relies on submarine cables and terrestrial fibre networks, limiting the impact of potential satellite disruptions as these systems serve only as secondary channels for specific enterprises. The Government will collaborate with industry partners to enhance security and resilience against evolving cybersecurity threats, while actively participating in international bodies like the ISO and IEEE to adopt relevant standards. Satellite operators and partners implement specific cybersecurity and resilience standards tailored to their use cases, with sensitive data transmission protected by stringent encryption policies. The Government monitors rapid technological trends in commercial space endeavours closely to ensure the continued security of the nation's digital connectivity infrastructure.

results.title: Show of Proof of Vaccination against Diseases by S Pass Holders
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while the Government is reviewing compulsory vaccination for all Pass holders, current law under the Infectious Diseases Act 1976 mandates vaccination for children in Singapore and requires foreign-born children aged 12 and below applying for long-term passes to submit proof of immunity for diphtheria and measles to the National Immunisation Registry. No timeline for extending this requirement to all Pass holders was provided.

results.title: Strengthening Hub-and-spoke Models for Mental Health Triaging in Hospitals
results.summary: Under the National Mental Health and Well-being Strategy, the government has established a Mental Health Tiered Care Model to route patients from emergency departments and specialist outpatient clinics to appropriate community-based or primary care services. The Ministry of Health introduced First Stops for Mental Health via mindline 1771, mindline.sg, Community Outreach Teams, and CHAT services to provide assessment, brief support, triage, and referrals, thereby reducing unnecessary attendance at acute emergency rooms. For individuals with severe or urgent needs, psychiatrists assess admission requirements, while suitable patients at specialist outpatient clinics can continue care at primary settings with trained counsellors and social workers.

results.title: Take-up Rate for LPA since August 2025 and Proposed Extension of Fee Waiver
results.summary: LPA registrations rose from 350,000 in August 2025 to 410,000 in January 2026. The Ministry of Social and Family Development has not yet decided on extending the LPA Form 1 fee waiver, which expires on 31 March 2026. Citizens are encouraged to register their LPA immediately to utilize the remaining waiver period. No further timeline or eligibility changes regarding the fee extension were announced during the session.

results.title: Timeline of Hydrogen-Ready Power Plant Adoption and Resulting Impact on Household and Business Electricity Tariffs
results.summary: The Minister stated that hydrogen is not currently viable as a substitute for natural gas for electricity production due to high production and transportation costs. Consequently, no timeline has been set for the adoption of hydrogen-ready power plants, and the Ministry will continue monitoring global market developments before making a decision. No projected impact on household or business electricity tariffs has been provided at this stage.

results.title: Total Amount Paid to Foreign University Partners on Curriculum Development and Licensing in Past 25 years
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that Autonomous Universities collaborate with international partners for education and research, with associated costs for overseas resources deemed commensurate with the value obtained. Partnerships evolve over time and may end naturally when they no longer provide mutual benefit. The Ministry ensures funding for such collaborations is justified by the development of new local capabilities and future positioning for the universities. Specific figures for spending over the past 25 years, evaluation metrics beyond initial curriculum transfers, and cost-benefit analysis results comparing maintained versus terminated partnerships were not provided in the response.

results.title: Total Fees Collected for Vehicle Entry and Goods Vehicle Permits in 2025 and Estimated Annual Collection after Fee Increase in 2027
results.summary: In 2025, approximately $130 million in Vehicle Entry Permit and Goods Vehicle Permit fees were collected, with amounts varying based on the number of foreign-registered vehicles entering Singapore. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that fee increases effective 1 January 2027 will impact future annual collections, though specific post-2027 estimates were not provided in the response. The transcript does not detail the magnitude of the 2027 fee increase or projected collection figures beyond the 2025 baseline.

results.title: Tracking Singapore-headquartered Global Technology Champions, Strategic National Capabilities and Locally Anchored IP as Outcomes from Government's R&D Spending
results.summary: The Minister for Finance addressed the query regarding national outcomes from public R&D investments in the Prime Minister's Office Committee of Supply speech delivered on 26 February 2026. The response covered expectations for Singapore-headquartered global technology champions, strategic national capabilities, and locally anchored intellectual property over the next five years. Specific metrics for tracking success to ensure meaningful economic impact were detailed in that speech. Further details on measurement frameworks were not provided in this transcript excerpt.
